title: Anthropic Announces Jobs Most at Risk From AI

paragraphs.english: The AI jobs apocalypse, long predicted by tech leaders and AI critics alike, has never felt closer. A major job market disruption has been rumored for years now, but given the latest swings on Wall Street and major layoffs roiling the tech industry, the alarm bells are starting to go off.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di AI jobs apocalypse, long predicted by tech leaders and AI critics alike, get never feel closer. A big job market disruption don dey rumored for years now, but give di latest swings on Wall Street and big layoffs roiling di tech industry, di alarm bells dey starte to go comot from.

paragraphs.english: It’s looking likely that not every profession will be hit in the same way, nor to the same extent — a topic that frontier AI companies have been studying intensely.
paragraphs.pidgin: E’s looking likely dat no every profession go be hit for di same way, nor to di same extent — a topic dat frontier AI companies don dey studying intensely.

paragraphs.english: Last week, while the company’s CEO was engaged in a major fight with the Pentagon, Anthropic released its latest findings about the “labor market impacts of AI.” The company found that while “AI is far from reaching its theoretical capability” and “actual coverage remains a fraction of what’s feasible,” many occupations are at higher risk of “AI displacement.”
paragraphs.pidgin: Last week, while di company’s CEO bin engaged for a big fight wit di Pentagon, Anthropic release im latest findings about di “labor market impacts of AI.” Di company find tok while “AI na far from reaching im theoretical capability” and “actual coverage remains a fraction of wetin’s feasible,” plenti occupations dey for higher risk of “AI displacement.”

paragraphs.english: By taking into consideration “theoretical [large language model] capability and real-world usage data,” the team concluded that computer programmers, customer service reps, data entry keyers, medical record specialists, and market research analysts were at the highest risk since they were the “most exposed” to AI’s capabilities.
paragraphs.pidgin: By taking into consideration “theoretical [big language model] capability and real-world usage data,” di team concluded dat computer programmers, customer service reps, data entry keyers, medical record specialists, and market research analysts bin dey for di highest risk since dem bin dey di “most exposed” to AI’s capabilities.

paragraphs.english: Other affected occupations include investment analysts, software quality assurance, and information security analysts, as well as computer user support specialists.
paragraphs.pidgin: Other affected occupations include investment analysts, software quality assurance, and information security analysts, and also computer user support specialists.

paragraphs.english: Anthropic found that workers in these most exposed professions are “more likely to be older, female, more educated, and higher-paid,” highlighting gaps around gender, education, and more as the effects of AI on the job market come into focus.
paragraphs.pidgin: Anthropic find tok workers for dis ones most exposed professions dey “more likely to be older, female, more educated, and higher-pay,” highlighting gaps around gender, education, and more as di effects of AI on di job market come into focus.

paragraphs.english: The finding echoes previous research that similarly found women-dominated occupations are more vulnerable to AI disruption in the workforce.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di finding echoes before research dat similarly find women-dominated occupations dey more vulnerable to AI disruption for di workforce.

paragraphs.english: On the other end of the spectrum, the least exposed categories include professions that rely on a physical presence, and include cooks, mechanics, lifeguards, bartenders, dishwashers, and dressing room attendants, according to Anthropic — noble careers, but not ones known for paying particularly well.
paragraphs.pidgin: On di other end of di spectrum, di least exposed categories include professions dat rely on a physical presence, and include cooks, mechanics, lifeguards, bartenders, dishwashers, and dressing room attendants, as Anthropic — noble careers, but no ones known for paying particularly well.

paragraphs.english: There’s nothing particularly new about the conclusion that programmers are far more at risk from being made redundant by AI tech. Silicon Valley titans have been laying off thousands of workers as they continue to double down on their sizable investments in data centers.
paragraphs.pidgin: There’s notin particularly new about di conclusion dat programmers dey far more for risk from being make redundant by AI tech. Silicon Valley titans don dey laying comot from thousands of workers as dem continue to double down on dem sizable investments for data centers.

paragraphs.english: Most recently, Twitter founder and Block CEO Jack Dorsey laid off nearly half the staff at his fintech company, citing “intelligence tools we’re creating” that are allowing for “smaller and flatter teams.”
paragraphs.pidgin: Most recently, Twitter founder and Block CEO Jack Dorsey laid comot from nearly half di staff for im fintech company, citing “intelligence tools we’re creating” dat dey allowe for “smaller and flatter teams.”

paragraphs.english: “Within the next year, I believe the majority of companies will reach the same conclusion and make similar structural changes,” Dorsey wrote in a statement on X-formerly-Twitter last month.
paragraphs.pidgin: “Inside di next year, I believe di majority of companies go reach di same conclusion and make similar structural changes,” Dorsey write for a statement on X-formerly-Twitter last month.

paragraphs.english: But how much advancements in AI tech are really contributing to the layoffs remains a subject of heated debate. Former Block employees, for instance, have come forward to claim that the Dorsey-led layoffs were more likely an instance of “corporate downsizing” after a rash of pandemic-era overhiring.
paragraphs.pidgin: But how plenti advancements for AI tech dey true true contributing to di layoffs remains a subject of heated debate. Former Block employees, for instance, get come forward to claim dat di Dorsey-led layoffs bin dey more likely an instance of “corporate downsizing” after a rash of pandemic-era overhiring.

paragraphs.english: As Anthropic itself admits, we are still nowhere near AI’s “theoretical capability,” meaning the wave of job losses may not be due to AI automation. Then there’s all of the noise that could make it extremely difficult to gauge how many jobs were actually lost to AI or due to other, unrelated factors, like rising geopolitical instability, for instance, triggering economic uncertainty.
paragraphs.pidgin: As Anthropic imself admits, we dey still nowhere near AI’s “theoretical capability,” meaning di wave of job losses may no be because of AI automation. Then there’s all of di noise dat fit make e extremely difficult to gauge how plenti jobs bin dey actually lose to AI or because of other, unrelated factors, like rising geopolitical instability, for instance, triggering economic uncertainty.

paragraphs.english: Nonetheless, the company’s foreboding forecast highlights a rapidly changing labor landscape as colleges and universities face an existential threat, undermining the value of degrees. Computer science and computer engineering students, in particular, could be in deep trouble as employers continue to double down on AI.
paragraphs.pidgin: Nonetheless, di company’s foreboding forecast highlights a rapidly changing labor landscape as colleges and universities face an existential threat, undermining di value of degrees. Computer science and computer engineering students, for particular, fit be for deep wahala as employers continue to double down on AI.

paragraphs.english: Instead, according to Anthropic’s analysis of Bureau of Labor Statistics data, they’d be better served becoming electricians, registered nurses, lawyers, and accountants — the occupations expected to grow the most between 2024 and 2034.
paragraphs.pidgin: Instead, as Anthropic’s analysis of Bureau of Labor Statistics data, dem’d be better served becoming electricians, registered nurses, lawyers, and accountants — di occupations expected to grow di most between 2024 and 2034.

paragraphs.english: More on AI and jobs: The AI Jobs Apocalypse Is Starting to Feel Real
paragraphs.pidgin: More on AI and jobs: Di AI Jobs Apocalypse dey Starte to Feel Real
